title: Warm Dense Croatian Turkey Stew
description: A hearty one-pot Croatian dish featuring turkey, made with a mortar and pestle, and gluten-free friendly, this stew is a perfect comfort food for a chilly evening, filled with floral and dense flavors.
instructions: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped celery and cook for 2 minutes, until tender.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes, and cook for 5 minutes, until they start to soften.
Add the turkey breast pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Using a mortar and pestle, pound the dried thyme into a fine powder.
Add the pounded thyme, salt, and black pepper to the pot, and stir to combine.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 25 minutes, until the turkey is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
